top_evidence: Used to study mixing in stratified Boussinesq flows and available potential energy
Includes cyclogeostrophy and Ekman components with geostrophy
Model captures large-scale to submesoscale surface circulation features including fronts
MCL-1.level_description: State-of-the-art physics with comprehensive process representation

top_evidence: High-resolution product capable of identifying small-scale features detected with finite-size Lyapunov exponents (FSLEs)
MCL-2.level_description: All relevant scales fully resolved for intended applications

top_evidence: Used to reconstruct wintertime conditions from summertime observations, indicating seasonal temporal resolution capability
MCL-3.level_description: Broader set of time scales represented

top_evidence: Circulation physics linked to biogeochemical/biological horizontal distributions
Used to analyze air-sea CO2 fluxes, implying coupling or interface with atmospheric exchanges
MCL-4.level_description: Off-line coupling to multiple models OR full coupling between 2 components

top_evidence: Circulation product validated by in situ drifters
Able to identify water mass pathways and mesoscale structures
MCL-5.level_description: Routine use for prediction in limited settings

MCL-6.level_description: Not scalable, limited to small systems

top_evidence: Validated by in situ drifters (observational constraint)
Provides physical state estimates (stratification/mixing) for diagnostic energy analysis
Used to reconstruct pseudo observations for periods with sparse historical coverage (winter)
MCL-8.level_description: Multi-observation DA over long records for limited systems

top_evidence: Explicit validation against in situ drifter data
Comparison against finite-size Lyapunov exponents (FSLEs) for front detection
MCL-10.level_description: Routine validation against multiple datasets for limited cases

top_evidence: Used to estimate air-sea CO2 fluxes (climate research application)
Used to explain phytoplankton community structure shifts (ecosystem research)
Used to analyze biogeochemical distributions during cruise campaigns
